metadata.source: Firestore export with source-backed audit updates

accuracyConvention.spec: Published manufacturer, standards, or formula-derived target rate for supported modes.
accuracyConvention.estimate: Practical storage estimate for variable, content-dependent, or recommendation-based codecs.

categories.name: Delivery & Streaming
categories.description: Next-generation codec with superior compression efficiency

codecs.name: AV1

variants.name: Main Profile
variants.notes: AV1 estimate; AV1 profiles describe bit-depth/chroma capability, not fixed quality tiers.

variants.name: High Profile
variants.description: Advanced AV1 profile with enhanced features
variants.notes: AV1 estimate; AV1 profiles describe bit-depth/chroma capability, not fixed quality tiers.

variants.name: Professional Profile
variants.description: Professional AV1 profile for high-end applications
variants.notes: AV1 estimate; AV1 profiles describe bit-depth/chroma capability, not fixed quality tiers.

codecs.description: Latest open-source codec with superior compression
codecs.workflowNotes: Future-proof codec with excellent compression efficiency. Slower encoding but significantly smaller file sizes.
codecs.notes: Delivery bitrates are platform recommendations or content-dependent estimates, not codec-standard requirements.

codecs.name: H.264 (AVC)

variants.name: Baseline Profile
variants.notes: Legacy H.264 Baseline estimate; modern platforms generally recommend High Profile.

variants.name: Main Profile
variants.description: Standard profile for broadcast and streaming
variants.notes: H.264 Main estimate derived from upload guidance; use High Profile for broad modern delivery.

variants.name: High Profile
variants.notes: YouTube SDR upload midpoint estimates split by standard and high frame rates.

codecs.description: Most widely supported codec for delivery
codecs.workflowNotes: Best for web streaming, social media, and general distribution. Excellent compatibility across all devices and platforms.
codecs.notes: Delivery bitrates are platform recommendations or content-dependent estimates, not codec-standard requirements.

codecs.name: H.265 (HEVC)

variants.name: Main Profile
variants.notes: HEVC/H.265 delivery estimate from Apple HLS and YouTube live guidance; actual rate depends on encoder/content/HDR.

variants.name: Main 10
variants.notes: HEVC/H.265 delivery estimate from Apple HLS and YouTube live guidance; actual rate depends on encoder/content/HDR.

variants.name: Main 4:2:2 10
variants.notes: HEVC/H.265 delivery estimate from Apple HLS and YouTube live guidance; actual rate depends on encoder/content/HDR.

codecs.description: Next-generation codec with superior compression efficiency
codecs.workflowNotes: Better compression than H.264, ideal for 4K/8K streaming. Requires more processing power but delivers smaller file sizes.
codecs.notes: Delivery bitrates are platform recommendations or content-dependent estimates, not codec-standard requirements.

codecs.name: VP9

variants.name: Profile 0
variants.notes: VP9 VOD estimate from Google settings; Profile 2 does not automatically require more bitrate than Profile 0.

variants.name: Profile 2
variants.notes: VP9 VOD estimate from Google settings; Profile 2 does not automatically require more bitrate than Profile 0.

codecs.description: Google's open-source codec for web streaming
codecs.workflowNotes: Used by YouTube and other Google services. Good compression efficiency with royalty-free licensing.
codecs.notes: Delivery bitrates are platform recommendations or content-dependent estimates, not codec-standard requirements.

categories.name: Professional Intermediate
categories.description: Industry standard for professional editing and color grading

codecs.name: Apple ProRes

variants.name: ProRes 422 Proxy
variants.description: Lowest quality for offline editing and proxies
variants.notes: Apple ProRes target data-rate table values; UHD scaled from DCI 4K by active raster area.

variants.name: ProRes 422 LT
variants.description: Lower quality for basic editing workflows
variants.notes: Apple ProRes target data-rate table values; UHD scaled from DCI 4K by active raster area.

variants.name: ProRes 422
variants.notes: Apple ProRes target data-rate table values; UHD scaled from DCI 4K by active raster area.

variants.name: ProRes 422 HQ
variants.notes: Apple ProRes target data-rate table values; UHD scaled from DCI 4K by active raster area.

variants.name: ProRes 4444
variants.description: Highest quality with alpha channel support
variants.notes: Apple ProRes target data-rate table values; UHD scaled from DCI 4K by active raster area.

variants.name: ProRes 4444 XQ
variants.description: Extreme quality for high-end color grading
variants.notes: Apple ProRes target data-rate table values; UHD scaled from DCI 4K by active raster area.

codecs.description: Industry standard for professional editing and color grading
codecs.workflowNotes: Optimized for editing performance with excellent image quality. Widely supported across professional NLEs and color grading systems.
codecs.notes: Apple ProRes target data rates; table values used where published, with UHD scaled from Apple HD/DCI targets.

codecs.name: Avid DNxHD/HR

variants.name: DNxHD 36
variants.description: Lowest quality DNxHD for offline editing
variants.notes: Documented DNxHD bitrate mode; unsupported combinations intentionally omitted.

variants.name: DNxHD 115
variants.notes: Documented DNxHD bitrate mode; unsupported combinations intentionally omitted.

variants.name: DNxHD 120
variants.notes: Documented DNxHD bitrate mode; unsupported combinations intentionally omitted.

variants.name: DNxHD 145
variants.description: Standard quality DNxHD for online editing
variants.notes: Documented DNxHD bitrate mode; unsupported combinations intentionally omitted.

variants.name: DNxHD 175
variants.description: High quality DNxHD for 23.98/24/25/29.97/30 workflows (10-bit variant is 175x)
variants.notes: Documented DNxHD bitrate mode; unsupported combinations intentionally omitted.

variants.name: DNxHR LB
variants.description: Low Bandwidth DNxHR for proxy workflows
variants.notes: Converted from Avid DNxHR bandwidth tables using HD 23.976 anchor values and raster/frame-rate scaling; SQ corrected from previous undercount.

variants.name: DNxHR SQ
variants.notes: Converted from Avid DNxHR bandwidth tables using HD 23.976 anchor values and raster/frame-rate scaling; SQ corrected from previous undercount.

variants.name: DNxHR HQ
variants.notes: Converted from Avid DNxHR bandwidth tables using HD 23.976 anchor values and raster/frame-rate scaling; SQ corrected from previous undercount.

variants.name: DNxHR HQX
variants.notes: Converted from Avid DNxHR bandwidth tables using HD 23.976 anchor values and raster/frame-rate scaling; SQ corrected from previous undercount.

variants.name: DNxHR 444
variants.notes: Converted from Avid DNxHR bandwidth tables using HD 23.976 anchor values and raster/frame-rate scaling; SQ corrected from previous undercount.

variants.name: DNxHD 185
variants.notes: Added from DNxHD naming/bandwidth references.

variants.name: DNxHD 185x
variants.notes: Added from DNxHD naming/bandwidth references.

variants.name: DNxHD 220
variants.notes: Added from DNxHD naming/bandwidth references.

variants.name: DNxHD 220x
variants.notes: Added from DNxHD naming/bandwidth references.

codecs.description: Avid's professional codec family optimized for editorial workflows
codecs.workflowNotes: Designed for Avid Media Composer and broadcast workflows. Excellent for collaborative editing environments.
codecs.notes: DNxHD entries are documented HD modes; DNxHR entries are converted from Avid bandwidth tables where available.

codecs.name: GoPro CineForm

variants.name: CineForm Low
variants.notes: Approximate constant-quality CineForm estimate using about 10:1 compression from 10-bit 4:2:2 uncompressed.

variants.name: CineForm Medium
variants.notes: Approximate constant-quality CineForm estimate using about 7:1 compression from 10-bit 4:2:2 uncompressed.

variants.name: CineForm High
variants.notes: Approximate constant-quality CineForm estimate using about 5:1 compression from 10-bit 4:2:2 uncompressed.

variants.name: CineForm Film Scan
variants.description: Highest quality for film scanning applications
variants.notes: Approximate constant-quality CineForm estimate using about 4:1 compression from 10-bit 4:2:2 uncompressed.

codecs.description: Wavelet-based intermediate codec for mixed resolution workflows
codecs.workflowNotes: Excellent for mixed resolution projects and cross-platform compatibility. Good balance of quality and file size.
codecs.notes: CineForm is constant-quality; bitrates vary by image content. Values are storage estimates from typical compression ratios.

categories.name: Camera Acquisition
categories.description: SD MPEG IMX and DVCAM, Professional Disc

codecs.name: Panasonic AVC-Intra

variants.name: AVC-Intra 50
variants.notes: Nominal AVC-Intra operating point by class and frame rate; exact support depends on AVC-ULTRA device/mode.

variants.name: AVC-Intra 100
variants.notes: Nominal AVC-Intra operating point by class and frame rate; exact support depends on AVC-ULTRA device/mode.

variants.name: AVC-Intra 200
variants.notes: Nominal AVC-Intra operating point by class and frame rate; exact support depends on AVC-ULTRA device/mode.

codecs.description: Panasonic's intraframe codec for professional cameras
codecs.workflowNotes: Used in Panasonic professional cameras. Intraframe compression for easy editing and color grading.
codecs.notes: AVC-Intra classes are nominal operating points; values vary by class/frame rate and unsupported SD/4K catchalls are omitted.

codecs.name: Sony XAVC

variants.name: XAVC-L
variants.description: Long GOP XAVC for efficient recording
variants.notes: Common XAVC operating-point estimate; exact support depends on profile/camera/system frequency.

variants.name: XAVC-I
variants.notes: Common XAVC operating-point estimate; exact support depends on profile/camera/system frequency.

variants.name: XAVC-HS
variants.notes: Common XAVC operating-point estimate; exact support depends on profile/camera/system frequency.

codecs.description: Sony's modern acquisition format for high-end cameras
codecs.workflowNotes: Used in Sony's professional and cinema cameras. Excellent image quality with efficient compression.
codecs.notes: XAVC operating points vary by Sony profile, camera, raster, and frame rate; values use common published operating points.

codecs.name: Sony XDCAM

variants.name: XDCAM EX HQ 35 Mb/s
variants.description: XDCAM EX: 35 Mb/s, 4:2:0, MP4, SxS. Supports SD fallback.
variants.notes: XDCAM EX HQ mode, VBR maximum 35 Mb/s. Family: Used for field acquisition, SxS cards, 4:2:0 chroma. Also known as XDCAM EX. Supports SD fallback.

variants.name: XDCAM EX SP 25 Mb/s
variants.description: XDCAM EX: 25 Mb/s, 4:2:0, MP4, SxS. Supports SD fallback.
variants.notes: XDCAM EX SP mode, CBR 25 Mb/s at 1440x1080 compatibility mode. Family: Used for field acquisition, SxS cards, 4:2:0 chroma. Also known as XDCAM EX. Supports SD fallback.

variants.name: XDCAM HD HQ 35 Mb/s
variants.description: XDCAM HD: 35 Mb/s, 4:2:0, MXF, Professional Disc. Supports SD fallback.
variants.notes: XDCAM HD HQ mode, VBR maximum 35 Mb/s. Family: Used for general HD acquisition, 4:2:0 chroma. Also known as XDCAM35 or XDCAM HD. Supports SD fallback.

variants.name: XDCAM HD SP 25 Mb/s
variants.description: XDCAM HD: 25 Mb/s, 4:2:0, MXF, Professional Disc. Supports SD fallback.
variants.notes: XDCAM HD SP/HDV-compatible 25 Mb/s mode. Family: Used for general HD acquisition, 4:2:0 chroma. Also known as XDCAM35 or XDCAM HD. Supports SD fallback.

variants.name: XDCAM HD LP 18 Mb/s
variants.description: XDCAM HD: 18 Mb/s, 4:2:0, MXF, Professional Disc. Supports SD fallback.
variants.notes: XDCAM HD LP mode, VBR maximum 18 Mb/s. Family: Used for general HD acquisition, 4:2:0 chroma. Also known as XDCAM35 or XDCAM HD. Supports SD fallback.

variants.name: XDCAM HD422 50 Mb/s
variants.description: XDCAM HD422: 50 Mb/s, 4:2:2, MXF, Professional Disc/SxS. Supports SD fallback.
variants.notes: XDCAM HD422 MPEG-2 4:2:2P@HL CBR 50 Mb/s mode. Family: Used for broadcast delivery, supports 4:2:2 chroma, 8 audio channels. Also known as XDCAM50 or XDCAM HD422.

variants.name: XDCAM SD IMX 50 Mb/s
variants.notes: SD-only XDCAM MPEG IMX/DVCAM mode. Family: Standard definition, IMX and DVCAM, 4:2:2 and 4:1:1. Also known as XDCAM SD.

variants.name: XDCAM SD IMX 40 Mb/s
variants.notes: SD-only XDCAM MPEG IMX/DVCAM mode. Family: Standard definition, IMX and DVCAM, 4:2:2 and 4:1:1. Also known as XDCAM SD.

variants.name: XDCAM SD IMX 30 Mb/s
variants.notes: SD-only XDCAM MPEG IMX/DVCAM mode. Family: Standard definition, IMX and DVCAM, 4:2:2 and 4:1:1. Also known as XDCAM SD.

variants.name: XDCAM SD DVCAM 25 Mb/s
variants.notes: SD-only XDCAM MPEG IMX/DVCAM mode. Family: Standard definition, IMX and DVCAM, 4:2:2 and 4:1:1. Also known as XDCAM SD.

codecs.description: Sony XDCAM recording families including XDCAM EX, XDCAM HD, XDCAM HD422, and XDCAM SD.
codecs.workflowNotes: Select Sony XDCAM first, then choose the XDCAM family and recording mode as the codec variant.
codecs.notes: Merged XDCAM families into one codec. XDCAM EX LP 18 Mb/s removed because Sony EX docs list HQ 35 VBR and SP 25 CBR; 18 Mb/s belongs to XDCAM HD Professional Disc.

categories.name: Broadcast
categories.description: Wavelet-based codec for high-end broadcast applications

codecs.name: JPEG 2000

variants.name: J2K Lossless
variants.notes: Lossless JPEG 2000 estimate; content dependent, scaled by active raster and frame rate.

variants.name: J2K Visually Lossless
variants.notes: Visually lossless JPEG 2000 estimate around 3 bits/pixel, scaled by raster and frame rate.

variants.name: J2K Lossy
variants.notes: High-quality lossy JPEG 2000 estimate around 1.5 bits/pixel, scaled by raster and frame rate.

variants.name: J2K IMF 4K
variants.notes: IMF/JPEG 2000 profile maximum estimate retained separately from DCP 250 Mbit/s 24 fps constraints.

codecs.description: Wavelet-based codec for high-end broadcast applications
codecs.workflowNotes: Used in high-end broadcast workflows and digital cinema distribution. Excellent image quality with scalable compression.
codecs.notes: Generic JPEG 2000 entries are bit-per-pixel estimates; IMF/DCP profiles should be used for strict delivery constraints.

codecs.name: MPEG-2

variants.name: MPEG-2 422P@ML
variants.notes: MPEG-2 4:2:2P@ML representative SD 50 Mb/s mode.

variants.name: MPEG-2 422P@HL 50 Mb/s Broadcast
variants.notes: Common MPEG-2 4:2:2P@HL broadcast target, not the 300 Mb/s profile maximum.

variants.name: MPEG-2 MP@ML
variants.notes: MPEG-2 Main Profile Main Level SD estimate.

codecs.description: Traditional broadcast standard still used in many applications
codecs.workflowNotes: Legacy format widely used in broadcast television, DVD, and satellite transmission. Still relevant for certain broadcast workflows.
codecs.notes: MPEG-2 profile entries represent common implementation targets or profile maximums, not universal codec defaults.

codecs.name: MXF D-10 (IMX)

variants.name: MXF D-10 / IMX 30
variants.description: SD-only SMPTE D-10 / MPEG IMX 30 Mb/s essence in MXF.
variants.notes: SMPTE D-10 / MPEG IMX is SD intraframe MPEG-2 4:2:2 at 30/40/50 Mb/s.

variants.name: MXF D-10 / IMX 40
variants.description: SD-only SMPTE D-10 / MPEG IMX 40 Mb/s essence in MXF.
variants.notes: SMPTE D-10 / MPEG IMX is SD intraframe MPEG-2 4:2:2 at 30/40/50 Mb/s.

variants.name: MXF D-10 / IMX 50
variants.description: SD-only SMPTE D-10 / MPEG IMX 50 Mb/s essence in MXF.
variants.notes: SMPTE D-10 / MPEG IMX is SD intraframe MPEG-2 4:2:2 at 30/40/50 Mb/s.

codecs.description: MXF D-10 format for professional broadcast and cinema workflows
codecs.workflowNotes: Used in professional broadcast and cinema workflows. Excellent for high-quality video and audio recording.
codecs.notes: SMPTE D-10 / MPEG IMX split into SD-only 30/40/50 Mb/s variants.

categories.name: RAW & Cinema
categories.description: Apple's RAW format combining RAW flexibility with ProRes efficiency

codecs.name: Arri RAW

variants.name: ARRIRAW 2.8K
variants.description: 2.8K resolution ARRIRAW for Alexa Classic and Amira
variants.notes: ARRIRAW camera-mode estimate updated from ARRI published data-rate references; use ARRI calculator for exact sensor mode.

variants.name: ARRIRAW 3.2K
variants.description: 3.2K resolution ARRIRAW for Alexa XT and Amira
variants.notes: ARRIRAW estimate; use ARRI Formats & Data Rate Calculator for exact project settings.

variants.name: ARRIRAW 4.5K
variants.description: 4.5K resolution ARRIRAW for Alexa SXT and Amira
variants.notes: ARRIRAW camera-mode estimate updated from ARRI published data-rate references; use ARRI calculator for exact sensor mode.

variants.name: ARRIRAW 6.5K
variants.description: 6.5K resolution ARRIRAW for Alexa LF and Mini LF
variants.notes: ARRIRAW camera-mode estimate updated from ARRI published data-rate references; use ARRI calculator for exact sensor mode.

variants.name: ARRIRAW HDE 2.8K
variants.notes: ARRIRAW HDE is lossless and typically about 60% of ARRIRAW size per ARRI guidance.

variants.name: ARRIRAW HDE 3.2K
variants.notes: ARRIRAW HDE is lossless and typically about 60% of ARRIRAW size per ARRI guidance.

variants.name: ARRIRAW HDE 4.5K
variants.notes: ARRIRAW HDE is lossless and typically about 60% of ARRIRAW size per ARRI guidance.

variants.name: ARRIRAW HDE 6.5K
variants.notes: ARRIRAW HDE is lossless and typically about 60% of ARRIRAW size per ARRI guidance.

variants.name: ARRICORE 2K
variants.description: ARRICORE compressed RAW for Alexa Classic and Amira
variants.notes: ARRIRAW estimate; use ARRI Formats & Data Rate Calculator for exact project settings.

variants.name: ARRICORE 4K
variants.description: ARRICORE compressed RAW for Alexa SXT and Amira
variants.notes: ARRIRAW estimate; use ARRI Formats & Data Rate Calculator for exact project settings.

variants.name: ARRICORE 1080p
variants.description: ARRICORE compressed RAW for HD workflows
variants.notes: ARRIRAW estimate; use ARRI Formats & Data Rate Calculator for exact project settings.

codecs.description: Arri's uncompressed RAW format for maximum quality and flexibility
codecs.workflowNotes: Industry standard RAW format used in high-end cinema production. Provides maximum color grading flexibility and image quality. Used in Alexa LF, Mini LF, and other Arri cameras.
codecs.notes: ARRIRAW values should be verified with the ARRI data-rate calculator for exact camera/sensor mode; HDE variants are normalized to about 60% of ARRIRAW.

codecs.name: Blackmagic RAW

variants.name: BRAW Q0
variants.notes: BRAW Q0 constant-quality upper estimate; actual rate is scene-dependent and should be treated as a range.

variants.name: BRAW Q5
variants.notes: BRAW Q5 constant-quality estimate; actual rate is scene-dependent and should be treated as a range.

variants.name: BRAW 3:1
variants.notes: BRAW storage estimate from 12-bit Bayer sensor data at roughly 3:1. Q modes vary with image content.

variants.name: BRAW 5:1
variants.notes: BRAW storage estimate from 12-bit Bayer sensor data at roughly 5:1. Q modes vary with image content.

variants.name: BRAW 8:1
variants.notes: BRAW storage estimate from 12-bit Bayer sensor data at roughly 8:1. Q modes vary with image content.

variants.name: BRAW 12:1
variants.notes: BRAW storage estimate from 12-bit Bayer sensor data at roughly 12:1. Q modes vary with image content.

codecs.description: Blackmagic Design's intelligent RAW format
codecs.workflowNotes: Intelligent RAW codec with excellent compression efficiency. Optimized for DaVinci Resolve workflows with real-time playback.
codecs.notes: Blackmagic RAW constant-bitrate ratios are derived from sensor data rate; Q modes are content-dependent estimates.

codecs.name: Canon Cinema RAW

variants.name: Cinema RAW Light LT
variants.notes: Published Canon Cinema RAW Light table values for representative C500 Mark II sensor modes.

variants.name: Cinema RAW Light ST
variants.notes: Published Canon Cinema RAW Light table values for representative C500 Mark II sensor modes.

variants.name: Cinema RAW Light HQ
variants.notes: Published Canon Cinema RAW Light table values for representative C500 Mark II sensor modes.

codecs.description: Canon's cinema RAW format for professional cameras
codecs.workflowNotes: Used in Canon's cinema cameras like the C300, C500, and C700 series. Optimized for Canon's color science and workflows.
codecs.notes: Canon Cinema RAW Light values use EOS C500 Mark II published 4096x2160 S35 and 2048x1080 S16 tables.

codecs.name: ProRes RAW

variants.name: ProRes RAW
variants.notes: ProRes RAW estimate from Blackmagic 4096x2160 30p ProRes RAW/RAW HQ data rates, scaled by raster and frame rate; content-dependent.

variants.name: ProRes RAW HQ
variants.notes: ProRes RAW estimate from Blackmagic 4096x2160 30p ProRes RAW/RAW HQ data rates, scaled by raster and frame rate; content-dependent.

codecs.description: Apple's RAW format combining RAW flexibility with ProRes efficiency
codecs.workflowNotes: Maintains maximum image quality and color grading flexibility while providing efficient workflows in Final Cut Pro and other NLEs.
codecs.notes: Apple describes ProRes RAW as content-dependent; estimates are bounded by ProRes target-rate families.

codecs.name: RED R3D

variants.name: REDCODE RAW 2:1
variants.notes: Approximate REDCODE 2:1 estimate derived from RED 8K 8:1 24p example; actual rate varies.

variants.name: REDCODE RAW 3:1
variants.notes: Approximate REDCODE 3:1 estimate derived from RED 8K 8:1 24p example; actual rate varies.

variants.name: REDCODE RAW 4:1
variants.notes: Approximate REDCODE 4:1 estimate derived from RED 8K 8:1 24p example; actual rate varies.

variants.name: REDCODE RAW 5:1
variants.notes: Approximate REDCODE 5:1 estimate derived from RED 8K 8:1 24p example; actual rate varies.

variants.name: REDCODE RAW 8:1
variants.notes: Approximate REDCODE 8:1 estimate derived from RED 8K 8:1 24p example; actual rate varies.

codecs.description: RED Digital Cinema's proprietary RAW format
codecs.workflowNotes: Professional cinema RAW with variable compression ratios. Industry standard for high-end film and commercial production.
codecs.notes: REDCODE estimates are derived from RED 8K 8:1 24p example and scaled by compression ratio/frame rate; actual data rates are camera/media/content-dependent.

categories.name: Archival & Preservation
categories.description: Raw uncompressed video for maximum quality preservation

codecs.name: FFV1 (in Matroska/MKV)

variants.name: FFV1 Version 3
variants.description: Most common version for archival use with variable bitrates
variants.notes: FFV1 Version 3 lossless estimate at about one-third of 10-bit 4:2:2 uncompressed; actual compression is content-dependent.

codecs.description: Lossless intra-frame codec designed for archival and preservation
codecs.workflowNotes: Preferred by archives for its lossless compression, error resilience, and open specification. Typically stored in Matroska (MKV) container.
codecs.notes: Archival lossless rates are either formula-derived uncompressed rates or content-dependent lossless compression estimates.

codecs.name: Lossless JPEG 2000

variants.name: Lossless J2K
variants.notes: Lossless JPEG 2000 is content-dependent; values estimate about 60% of 10-bit 4:2:2 uncompressed.

codecs.description: Mathematically lossless JPEG 2000 for archival applications
codecs.workflowNotes: Provides lossless compression with better efficiency than uncompressed while maintaining mathematical integrity for archival purposes.
codecs.notes: Archival lossless rates are either formula-derived uncompressed rates or content-dependent lossless compression estimates.

codecs.name: Uncompressed Video

variants.name: 10-bit 4:2:2
variants.notes: Formula-derived active-picture 10-bit 4:2:2 rate: width x height x 20 bits x frame rate.

variants.name: 8-bit 4:2:2
variants.notes: Formula-derived active-picture 8-bit 4:2:2 rate: width x height x 16 bits x frame rate.

codecs.description: Raw uncompressed video for maximum quality preservation
codecs.workflowNotes: Used for master archival copies where storage space is not a concern. Provides bit-perfect preservation of original content.
codecs.notes: Archival lossless rates are either formula-derived uncompressed rates or content-dependent lossless compression estimates.
